metadata.author: Core Team
metadata.description: Open sandbox level with one stack, one queue, and one list.

metadata.author: Core Team
metadata.description: Solve the basic Tower of Hanoi using three stacks.

metadata.author: Core Team
metadata.description: Place the stack block, use its only available operation, and run it. The goal is simply to remove the only element in the stack.

introduces: colocar un bloque de estructura, seleccionar una operación y ejecutarla

messages.title: Place the stack block and use its operation
messages.body: Look at the initial and goal boards first. Stack A has only one element, and the goal is to remove it. Drag structure A into the program, open its action menu, choose the only available operation, then press Play and watch what happens. This is how the software applies actions to data structures.

messages.title: Open the action menu and run the stack operation
messages.body: This first level only uses one stack action. Place A, open its menu, select the available operation, and run the program to remove the single element.

messages.title: Match the exact visible difference
messages.body: The goal board is an empty stack. Use the stack action available in the block, then run again and observe the board.

introduces: colocar un bloque de estructura, seleccionar una operación y ejecutarla

messages.title: Place the stack block and use its operation
messages.body: Look at the initial and goal boards first. Stack A has only one element, and the goal is to remove it. messages.title: Open the action menu and run the stack operation
messages.body: This first level only uses one stack action. Place A, open its menu, select the available operation, and run the program to remove the single element.

messages.title: Match the exact visible difference
messages.body: The goal board is an empty stack. Use the stack action available in the block, then run again and observe the board.

metadata.author: Core Team
metadata.description: Use Step to watch two pop actions one by one, and use Reset to return to the initial stack and try again.

introduces: ejecución paso a paso y reinicio mientras repites una acción de pila

messages.title: Use Step and Reset with one stack
messages.body: This level uses only stack A. The goal is to empty it by applying the same action twice. Build that short solution, then use Step to watch the stack change one move at a time and Reset to return to the beginning.

messages.title: Check the stack after the first move
messages.body: After one Step, stack A should still contain one element. Use that intermediate state to confirm whether your first action is correct before finishing the level.

messages.title: This level repeats the same move twice
messages.body: You do not need a second structure here. Place the stack action twice, inspect the board after each Step, and use Reset whenever you want to retry from the original state.

introduces: ejecución paso a paso y reinicio mientras repites una acción de pila

messages.title: Use Step and Reset with one stack
messages.body: This level uses only stack A. The goal is to empty it by applying the same action twice. Build that short solution, then use Step to watch the stack change one move at a time and Reset to return to the beginning.

messages.title: Check the stack after the first move
messages.body: After one Step, stack A should still contain one element. Use that intermediate state to confirm whether your first action is correct before finishing the level.

messages.title: This level repeats the same move twice
messages.body: You do not need a second structure here. Place the stack action twice, inspect the board after each Step, and use Reset whenever you want to retry from the original state.

metadata.author: Core Team
metadata.description: Read the level description, use queue A with enqueue, and drag the literal block from the left palette to insert item.

introduces: usar la descripción del nivel, la paleta izquierda y la salida para completar un nivel
reinforces: literal blocks can complete an action

messages.title: Use the description, blocks, and output
messages.body: This level closes the onboarding. Read the description first, place queue A, choose enqueue, and use the literal block from the left palette to insert item. If something is missing, the output area will tell you.

messages.title: The output tells you what is missing
messages.body: If the queue did not change, read the output area before trying random edits. In this level, the most likely issue is that enqueue still needs its value.

messages.title: Use the literal the level asked for
messages.body: The level description already tells you the exact value to insert. Drag the literal block from the left palette into the empty slot of the queue action, then run again.

introduces: usar la descripción del nivel, la paleta izquierda y la salida para completar un nivel
reinforces: literal blocks can complete an action

messages.title: Use the description, blocks, and output
messages.body: This level closes the onboarding. Read the description first, place queue A, choose enqueue, and use the literal block from the left palette to insert item. If something is missing, the output area will tell you.

messages.title: The output tells you what is missing
messages.body: If the queue did not change, read the output area before trying random edits. In this level, the most likely issue is that enqueue still needs its value.

messages.title: Use the literal the level asked for
messages.body: The level description already tells you the exact value to insert. Drag the literal block from the left palette into the empty slot of the queue action, then run again.

metadata.author: Core Team
metadata.description: A short autonomous checkpoint: compare the boards, choose the correct move order, and solve the full mini challenge by yourself.

introduces: resolución autónoma de un reto corto usando el flujo completo del editor

messages.title: Time for a small independent challenge
messages.body: This level combines the workflow from the previous onboarding levels. Read both boards, decide the correct order of moves, then solve it without step-by-step guidance.

messages.title: Order matters now
messages.body: The target stack C must receive values in the right order. If the result is reversed, check which transfer should happen first.

messages.title: Use the whole routine you learned
messages.body: Compare, plan, run, and correct. This checkpoint is not introducing a new tool; it is checking that you can use the editor flow on your own.

introduces: resolución autónoma de un reto corto usando el flujo completo del editor

messages.title: Time for a small independent challenge

messages.title: Use the whole routine you learned
messages.body: Compare, plan, run, and correct. This checkpoint is not introducing a new tool; it is checking that you can use the editor flow on your own.

metadata.author: Core Team
metadata.description: Learn stack basics: use POP on A and PUSH into B to move the top element.

metadata.author: Core Team
metadata.description: Practice repetition: empty A with POP and build B from scratch using PUSH only.

metadata.author: Core Team
metadata.description: Mix default data structures: reverse queue Q using stack S as auxiliary memory.

metadata.author: Core Team
metadata.description: Learn list edge operations: trim exactly one item from each side of L.

metadata.author: Core Team
metadata.description: Create a helper function (for example moveOne) and reuse it to transfer A into B.

metadata.author: Core Team
metadata.description: Define a custom function and reuse it to move values from stack A into queue Q.

metadata.author: Core Team
metadata.description: Create a custom type (example: Box), then solve the stack transfer using your own model objects if you want.

title: Campaign 08 · Integrate Custom Type + Default DS

metadata.author: Core Team
metadata.description: Final challenge: solve with default structures and optionally model helper data using a custom type.

metadata.author: Core Team
metadata.description: Introduce la semántica FIFO moviendo el frente de una cola al final de otra.

introduces: Comportamiento FIFO en cola mediante DEQUEUE y ENQUEUE

messages.title: Identifica qué sale primero
messages.body: En una cola no trabajas con el tope, sino con el frente. Observa A y mueve exactamente el primer valor hacia B para que el resto conserve su orden.

messages.title: El orden de llegada importa
messages.body: Si A no quedó con 2 y 3 en ese orden, entonces la cola fue manipulada como si fuera otra estructura. Recuerda: sale el frente y entra por el final.

messages.title: Haz solo el cambio necesario
messages.body: La meta pide un movimiento muy específico: el primer elemento de A debe terminar como único elemento en B, sin alterar el orden restante de A.

introduces: Comportamiento FIFO en cola mediante DEQUEUE y ENQUEUE

messages.title: Identifica qué sale primero
messages.body: En una cola no trabajas con el tope, sino con el frente. Observa A y mueve exactamente el primer valor hacia B para que el resto conserve su orden.

messages.title: El orden de llegada importa
messages.body: Si A no quedó con 2 y 3 en ese orden, entonces la cola fue manipulada como si fuera otra estructura. Recuerda: sale el frente y entra por el final.

messages.title: Haz solo el cambio necesario
messages.body: La meta pide un movimiento muy específico: el primer elemento de A debe terminar como único elemento en B, sin alterar el orden restante de A.

metadata.author: Core Team
metadata.description: Practica una rotación simple de cola: el frente se mueve al final sin invertir el orden de los demás elementos.

introduces: Rotación controlada de una cola preservando FIFO

messages.title: Rota la cola sin romper su lógica
messages.body: La meta no invierte la cola. Solo mueve el frente actual al final y deja que los demás valores avancen una posición.

messages.title: Rotar no es invertir
messages.body: Si el orden quedó al revés, entonces la estrategia ya no respeta el comportamiento natural de una cola. Busca un cambio circular, no una inversión.

messages.title: Piensa qué valor debe quedar último
messages.body: El 1 ya no debe estar primero: debe terminar al final. Todo lo demás conserva el mismo orden relativo que ya tenía.
